findings.evidence: `TotallyReal.lean:66-67` sets `[Ring R]`; Mathlib defines `LinearEquiv.skewSwap` under `[Semiring R] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup N]` at `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Prod.lean:701-706`, and linear-map negation is available under semirings with additive-group codomain at `Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LinearMap/Defs.lean:775-779,853-864`.
findings.fix: Change this section to `variable [Semiring R] [AddCommGroup E] [Module R E]`; keep only genuinely ring-dependent lemmas in a separate ring section if any remain.
findings.issue: The whole `AddCommGroup` section assumes `[Ring R]`, making `map_map_eq_of_comp_self_eq_neg_id`, `IsTotallyReal.image`, `image_iff`, and the `skewSwap` factor lemmas less general than the APIs they use.

summary: The basic API is now at semiring level, but the image and doubled-module lemmas still impose a ring of scalars where Mathlib’s relevant linear-map and skew-swap APIs only need a semiring with additive groups.
